card.intuition: An extension of TDD that focuses on the 'behavior' of the system from a business perspective, using natural language (Given/When/Then) to define requirements.
card.analogy: A contract between a customer and a builder: instead of saying 'Use 50 bricks', the contract says 'GIVEN I am standing outside, WHEN I open the door, THEN I should see a kitchen'. It describes the result, not the implementation.
when_to_use: Collaborating with non-technical stakeholders (PO, QA) to define acceptance criteria
Building complex user flows (e.g., 'A user can checkout if they have at least 3 items')
Ensuring that the software actually delivers what the business needs
gotchas: Complexity: BDD frameworks like Cucumber/Gherkin can be slow to set up and maintain. Don't use it for simple unit tests.
English is ambiguous: if your 'Then' statements aren't precise, the automated tests will be flaky.

exercise.prompt: Identify the three keywords used in Gherkin syntax to define a BDD scenario.
exercise.test_code:
assert "given" in solution.lower()
assert "when" in solution.lower()
assert "then" in solution.lower()
print("PASS")
